summary: Hotz laments the death of authentic counterculture in technology, arguing that the rebellious spirit of punk rock and dropout entrepreneurs has been completely co-opted by the system. He contends that modern tech startups following the VC playbook are merely 'underpaid research divisions of big tech companies' rather than genuine innovators. The post questions whether meaningful resistance to corporate conformity is even possible anymore, comparing the situation to a decentralized Truman Show where everyone participates in their own oppression. His only hope lies in the possibility that centralized, bureaucratic tech companies can only produce failures like the 737 MAX, while genuine innovation might still come from kids building things in garages just because they can.
key_points: Mainstream punk rock preached anti-conformity while being fully absorbed by the system it claimed to oppose
Modern 'dropout entrepreneurs' like those following Zuckerberg's path are just pursuing system-granted status through a different route
Tech has been gentrified for the 'professional managerial class,' with authentic hacker culture nearly eradicated
Cultural conformity is now decentralized - 'a little Goebbels inside everyone with a reddit account' - making it harder to resist
The outcome of self-driving cars may determine whether centralized corporate tech or decentralized garage hackers control the future
money_quotes: I wish tattoos still made you unemployable. Then I'd get some.
Drop out in pursuit of more system granted status and wealth. Fuck you.
YOU ARE PART OF THE SYSTEM. YOU ARE NOT INNOVATING. YOU ARE JUST UNDERPAID RESEARCH DIVISIONS OF BIG TECH COMPANIES.
Gentrifying pieces of shit, making technology safe for the professional managerial class.
The modern state doesn't need a Goebbels. There's a little Goebbels inside every - with a reddit account.
We are all doing this to ourselves. It's a decentralized cultural revolution.
But perhaps they can only build 737 MAXes, and legit kids in their garages will build good flying cars not for any reason but because they can.
What would I buy with money?
key_insight: True technological rebellion has been co-opted into a conformist VC-funded pipeline, and the only hope for authentic innovation lies in people building things purely for the joy of creation rather than for money or status.
